threeMenInABoat.note: Long Reading Text (Novel) by Jerome K. Jerome — CBSE Class 9

chapters.title: Three Men in a Boat — Novel Overview
chapters.type: Novel by Jerome K. Jerome (Long Reading Text)
topics: Three friends — J. (narrator), George, and Harris — along with their dog Montmorency, take a comic boating trip up the River Thames
The story opens with the three 'hypochondriacs' discussing their imaginary ailments before deciding a holiday will cure them
Humorous mishaps include struggles with an oil stove, getting lost in Hampton Court Maze, and troubles opening a tin of pineapple
Digressions into local history along the Thames (Kingston, Hampton Court, Magna Carta island) blend travelogue with comedy
Central themes: friendship, humour, the escape from modern civilisation, and finding joy in simple pleasures
Known for its blend of slapstick comedy with reflective, almost poetic passages about nature

gulliversTravels.note: Long Reading Text (Novel) by Jonathan Swift — CBSE Class 9 (often abridged Parts I & II: Lilliput and Brobdingnag)

chapters.title: Gulliver's Travels — Novel Overview
chapters.type: Novel by Jonathan Swift (Long Reading Text)
topics: Part I — A Voyage to Lilliput: Gulliver is shipwrecked among six-inch-tall people; observes their petty political conflicts (Big-Endians vs Little-Endians)
Part II — A Voyage to Brobdingnag: Gulliver becomes a tiny curiosity among 60-foot giants, exposing human pettiness by comparison
Part III — Laputa and surrounding lands: satire on impractical scientists, philosophers, and 'projectors' detached from reality
Part IV — The Houyhnhnms: rational horses contrasted with brutish Yahoos, Swift's sharpest satire on human nature
Central themes: political satire, human folly and pride, relativity of size and perspective, critique of 18th-century English society
Gulliver serves as an everyman narrator whose journeys mirror Swift's broader commentary on politics, science, and morality

annefrank.bookName: The Diary of a Young Girl (Anne Frank)
annefrank.note: Long Reading Text (Autobiography/Diary) by Anne Frank — supplementary reading for Class 10

chapters.title: The Diary of a Young Girl — Book Overview
chapters.type: Diary/Autobiography by Anne Frank (Long Reading Text)
topics: Anne Frank's real diary written while hiding from Nazi persecution in Amsterdam (1942–1944)
Life in the secret 'Annexe' with her family and others, and the daily fears of discovery
Anne's reflections on adolescence, identity, family relationships, and hope amid horror
Historical context of the Holocaust and Nazi occupation of the Netherlands
The diary ends abruptly before her capture; she died in a concentration camp in 1945
Enduring themes: courage, the power of writing, resilience of the human spirit, and the tragedy of war

storyOfMyLife.bookName: The Story of My Life (Helen Keller)
storyOfMyLife.note: Long Reading Text (Autobiography) by Helen Keller — supplementary reading for Class 10

chapters.title: The Story of My Life — Book Overview
chapters.type: Autobiography by Helen Keller (Long Reading Text)
topics: Helen Keller's account of losing sight and hearing after a childhood illness
Her breakthrough moment learning language through teacher Anne Sullivan (the famous 'water' scene)
Her education and remarkable achievement of graduating from Radcliffe College
Overcoming immense physical challenges through determination and support
Themes: triumph of the human will, the transformative power of education, disability and resilience

cantervilleGhost.note: Long Reading Text (Novella) by Oscar Wilde — supplementary reading for Class 10

chapters.title: The Canterville Ghost — Novella Overview
chapters.type: Novella by Oscar Wilde (Long Reading Text)
topics: An American family moves into a haunted English mansion and refuses to be scared of its resident ghost
Comic reversal: the pragmatic, modern Americans outwit and humiliate the traditional ghost with their unsentimental attitude
Virginia, the young daughter, eventually helps the ghost find peace and release from his centuries-old curse
Blend of gothic horror parody with romance and redemption
Themes: clash of old-world tradition versus modernity, forgiveness, innocence, and spiritual redemption

upFromSlavery.note: Long Reading Text (Autobiography) by Booker T. Washington — supplementary reading for Class 10

chapters.title: Up From Slavery — Book Overview
chapters.type: Autobiography by Booker T. Washington (Long Reading Text)
topics: Booker T. Washington's journey from childhood enslavement to becoming a renowned educator
His struggle for education despite poverty and racial discrimination in the post-Civil War American South
Founding and building the Tuskegee Institute for African-American education
His philosophy of self-reliance, vocational education, and gradual social progress
Themes: perseverance, the transformative power of education, racial uplift, and personal dignity

houndOfBaskervilles.note: Long Reading Text (Novel) by Arthur Conan Doyle — supplementary reading for Class 11

chapters.title: The Hound of the Baskervilles — Novel Overview
chapters.type: Novel by Arthur Conan Doyle (Long Reading Text)
topics: Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson investigate a supposed supernatural curse afflicting the Baskerville family on Dartmoor
Sir Charles Baskerville's mysterious death and the arrival of his heir, Sir Henry, into a web of danger
Holmes's methodical deduction gradually unmasks a very human, calculated murder plot disguised as a legendary curse
Atmospheric gothic setting of the foggy moor heightens suspense throughout the mystery
Themes: reason versus superstition, greed and inheritance, classic detective fiction conventions

invisibleMan.note: Long Reading Text (Science Fiction Novel) by H.G. Wells — supplementary reading for Class 11

chapters.title: The Invisible Man — Novel Overview
chapters.type: Novel by H.G. Wells (Long Reading Text)
topics: Griffin, a scientist, discovers a method to become invisible but cannot reverse the process
His descent into isolation, paranoia, and increasingly violent behaviour as society rejects him
Conflict between Griffin and the villagers/authorities as his invisibility is exploited for crime and terror
His eventual downfall and death, becoming visible again only in death
Themes: the corrupting effect of unchecked power, isolation, scientific responsibility, and the double-edged nature of ambition

silasMarner.note: Long Reading Text (Novel) by George Eliot — supplementary reading for Class 12

chapters.title: Silas Marner — Novel Overview
chapters.type: Novel by George Eliot (Long Reading Text)
topics: Silas Marner, a weaver, is falsely accused of theft and exiled from his community, becoming a bitter recluse obsessed with hoarding gold
His gold is stolen, leaving him devastated, until an orphaned toddler named Eppie mysteriously appears at his hearth
Raising Eppie transforms Silas from an isolated miser into a loving, socially connected father figure
Parallel plot involving Godfrey Cass, Eppie's biological father, and his own guilt and eventual reckoning
Themes: redemption through love, community and belonging, the corrupting versus redemptive power of relationships versus material wealth

topics: Griffin, a scientist, discovers a method to become invisible but cannot reverse the process
His descent into isolation, paranoia, and increasingly violent behaviour as society rejects him
Conflict between Griffin and the villagers/authorities as his invisibility is exploited for crime and terror
His eventual downfall and death, becoming visible again only in death
Themes: the corrupting effect of unchecked power, isolation, scientific responsibility, and the double-edged nature of ambition
